purpose: Provide non-authoritative starter examples for MAS-owned publication-route memory migration without storing real study artifacts or accepted memory entries in the repo.

seed_cards.title: Weak internal effect can still be publishable when external validation or model update is clinically grounded.
seed_cards.prose_summary: Consider whether a route can shift from claiming discovery to validating transportability, recalibration, or clinically interpretable updating when the primary effect is weak but the endpoint, cohort, and clinical decision point remain relevant.
failure_modes: No independent cohort or temporal split can support transportability.
Calibration or clinical utility evidence is absent.
The revised claim still reads as discovery rather than validation.

seed_cards.title: Negative or unstable main analysis should trigger claim downgrade, bounded repair, route switch, or stop-loss rather than positive-story inflation.
seed_cards.prose_summary: When a bounded analysis campaign returns unstable, reversed, or non-identifiable evidence, preserve failed paths and let the controller decide whether to downgrade the claim, repair a bounded evidence gap, switch route, return to scouting, ask for a human gate, or stop.
failure_modes: The proposed memory repeats a single-study result instead of a reusable route lesson.
The proposed writeback tries to replace evidence or review ledgers.
The revised paper narrative hides the failed path.
